author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
viṣaya⋅vāsanārūpa cittera vikāra
āmāra hṛdaye bhoga kare anivāra

kata je jatana āmi karilāma hāya
nā gela vikāra buddhi śeṣe prāṇa jāya

e ghora vikāra more karila asthira
śānti nā pāila sthāna, antara adhīra

śrī⋅rūpa gosvāmī more kṛpā vitariyā
uddhāribe kabe jukta-vairāgya arpiyā

kabe sanātana more chāḍāye viṣaya
nityānande samarpibe hoiyā sadaya

śrī⋅jīva gosvāmī kabe siddhānta⋅salile
nibhāibe tarkānala, citta jāhe jvale

śrī⋅caitanya⋅nāma śune udibe pulaka
rādhā⋅kṛṣṇāmṛta⋅pāne hoiba aśoka

kāṅgālera sukāṅgāla durjana e⋅jana
vaiṣṇava⋅caraṇāśraya jāce akiñcana
en_translation:
The distortions of the mind in the form of material desires In my heart, they enjoy uncontrollably.

Alas, how much effort I made! The distorted intelligence did not leave, and finally my life departs.

These terrible distortions made me restless. Peace found no place, my inner self is agitated.

When will Śrī Rūpa Gosvāmī bestow mercy upon me, and deliver me by offering appropriate renunciation?

When will Sanātana Gosvāmī free me from material objects, and, becoming merciful, surrender me to Śrī Nityānanda Prabhu?

When will Śrī Jīva Gosvāmī, with the waters of established philosophical conclusions, extinguish the fire of arguments, in which my mind burns?

Hearing the name of Śrī Caitanya Mahāprabhu, when will ecstasy arise, and by drinking the nectar of Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa, when will I become free from sorrow?

This person is a wicked soul, poorer than the poorest, a humble soul who begs for the shelter of the feet of the devotees of Viṣṇu.
